steps.title: Set up the equation using the given condition
steps.content:
We're given that $f(8) = 49 \cdot f(6)$. Substituting $f(x) = c^x$:
$$c^8 = 49 \cdot c^6$$

steps.title: Apply the exponent quotient rule
steps.content:
Divide both sides by $c^6$ (valid since $c > 1$):
$$\frac{c^8}{c^6} = 49$$
This simplifies to:
$$c^2 = 49$$
